### Where We Stopped
Migration is complete. Both repos are clean. The one remaining item is the end-to-end redirect verification (needs main site redeployed to test `/ab-simulator/` → `absim.eeshans.com`).

## Decisions Made

| Decision | Rationale | Files |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| Notebook writes to Supabase, not YAML | Eliminates git-commit-from-CI, enables meta-analysis | notebooks/ab_test_analysis.ipynb |
| `ab_simulator_summary` appends rows | Time series of experiment evolution | supabase migration |
| `user_rank` RPC for exact rank | Leaderboard RPC only returns top 10 | Supabase RPC |
| Client-side NotebookSummary | Static site stays fresh without rebuilds | NotebookSummary.astro |
| Personal best = celebration modal | Old pill was squeezed, hard to see | personal-best.js, app.css |
| Notebook dark mode via CSS invert | Jupyter has complex CSS, invert+hue-rotate handles everything | analysis/index.astro |
| fly.toml keeps public keys | Consistent with datascienceapps, keys are public by design | fly.toml |
| No shared design package | CSS vars + fonts copy is simpler for 2-3 consumers | app.css |
| Project YAML = static metadata only | Dynamic stats come from Supabase, not hardcoded strings | projects/*.yaml |
| Wait for user verification before committing | User feedback — don't commit after saying "check dev server" | memory/feedback_wait_before_commit.md |

## Key Architecture

### AB Simulator Pipeline
```
GitHub Actions (every 2h) → jupyter execute → writes to Supabase
Website → client-side fetch from Supabase → always fresh
Push to main → GitHub Actions → fly deploy → live
```

### Shared Infrastructure
- Supabase: `nazioidbiydxduonenmb` (soma-ab-test)
- PostHog: proxied through `api-v2.eeshans.com` Cloudflare Worker
- Feature flag: `word_search_difficulty_v2`

## Next Actions

1. Deploy main site (datascienceapps) to verify `/ab-simulator/` redirect works
2. Main site merge to `main` — all phases complete, ready for final review
3. Consider similar migration for How I Prompt project
4. Lighthouse audit, cross-browser testing (deferred)

## Conversation Essence

- User strongly prefers simplicity — drove the 7→3 layer notebook pipeline simplification
- User catches when Claude fabricates data (evidence fields) — always verify against source
- Never commit before user verifies on dev server (feedback saved to memory)
- Never push without explicit approval
- User is building for O-1 visa — projects must be authoritative
- Public keys in fly.toml are fine — consistent with datascienceapps pattern
- User prefers Fly.io for hosting (already using it)
- Theme system (6 themes) is a distinctive feature user is proud of
- `superfly/flyctl-actions` only has `@master` tag, no `@v2`
